title: Clean up test_stages.py: redundant import, misleading test names, meta-tests
analysis:
The task addresses four review findings from task 142 on fused-memory/tests/test_stages.py:

1. **Redundant AsyncMock imports**: There are 7 local `from unittest.mock import AsyncMock` scattered across test classes/methods (lines 67, 253, 285, 331, 426, 589, 601). No module-level import exists currently — the task description references a module-level import on 'line 4' that must have existed in an earlier version. Fix: add a single module-level `from unittest.mock import AsyncMock` and remove all 7 local copies.

2. **TestSharedFixtureContract removal**: This class no longer exists in the file — likely removed in commit e2fce78 ('simplify: remove dead code, premature abstractions, and over-engineering'). No action needed.

3. **test_default_limits misleading name**: Creates MemoryConsolidator with ReconciliationConfig() defaults and asserts episode_limit==500 and memory_limit==1000. These are CLASS-level defaults on MemoryConsolidator, which happen to match the opus tier (500/1000), NOT the framework config defaults (sonnet: 125/250). Rename to test_tier_limits_with_default_class_values.

4. **test_sonnet_tier_limits misleading name**: Despite its name, this test only verifies that episode_limit and memory_limit attributes are mutable (sets 125/250 then asserts). It does not exercise _select_tier() or verify actual sonnet tier selection. Rename to test_limits_are_mutable.

This is a pure refactoring task on test code — no production code changes. The TDD pattern is: verify green baseline → make atomic refactoring change → verify green after each change.

prerequisites.description: Run existing test_stages.py to establish green baseline — all tests must pass before refactoring begins

steps.description: Consolidate AsyncMock imports: add `from unittest.mock import AsyncMock` at module level (after the pytest import, line 5), then remove all 7 local `from unittest.mock import AsyncMock` at lines 67, 253, 285, 331, 426, 589, 601. Keep the `from unittest.mock import patch` on line 446 as-is since it is a separate import used only once (out of task scope).
Run full test_stages.py suite to verify import consolidation did not break anything. All tests must pass identically to the baseline.

Rename TestTierConfig.test_default_limits → test_tier_limits_with_default_class_values. Add docstring: 'MemoryConsolidator class defaults (500/1000) match opus tier, not ReconciliationConfig sonnet defaults (125/250).'
Run TestTierConfig::test_tier_limits_with_default_class_values to verify the renamed test passes. Confirm the old name test_default_limits no longer exists.
Rename TestTierConfig.test_sonnet_tier_limits → test_limits_are_mutable. Add docstring: 'episode_limit and memory_limit are mutable — the harness sets them from TierConfig before each run.'
Run full test_stages.py suite as final verification. All tests must pass. Confirm both old test names (test_default_limits, test_sonnet_tier_limits) are gone and replaced by the new names.

design_decisions.decision: Rename test_sonnet_tier_limits → test_limits_are_mutable (not replace with _select_tier test)
design_decisions.rationale: _select_tier() lives on ReconciliationHarness, not on stage classes. Adding a _select_tier() test to test_stages.py would be a scope violation — it belongs in test_harness.py. The current test body (set attribute, assert value) genuinely tests mutability, so the rename accurately describes what it does.

design_decisions.decision: Keep `from unittest.mock import patch` as a local import on line 446
design_decisions.rationale: The task specifically calls out AsyncMock redundancy. The `patch` import is used in only one location (TestProjectIdValidation._patch_stage) and was not flagged in the review. Touching it would exceed task scope.
This class no longer exists in the file — it was removed in a prior commit (likely e2fce78). The review finding is already resolved.

design_decisions.decision: Add docstrings to renamed tests rather than inline comments
design_decisions.rationale: Docstrings appear in pytest verbose output and IDE tooltips, making the intent visible where it matters. An inline comment only helps when reading the source.

reuse.how: Add AsyncMock import in the same style — stdlib imports grouped together (json, unittest.mock), then pytest, then project imports.
Rename methods in-place, preserving the existing test body and assertions. Only add docstrings and change method names.
